contract.name: v2/autocomplete/accounts/ata.md
contract.description: Returns Allocation Transfer Agency (ATA) suggestions for Treasury account autocomplete in advanced search.

filters.description: Optional object containing treasury account component filters; only recognized keys are applied.

ata.description: ATA prefix to match. Provide a non-empty string to prefix-match three-digit ATA codes or null to include accounts missing an ATA.
ata.constraints: Strings must contain at least one character; numeric values are rejected with a 400 error.

aid.description: Agency Identifier exact match or null to include accounts missing an AID.
aid.constraints: Strings must be non-empty; empty strings trigger a 422 validation error.

bpoa.description: Beginning Period of Availability exact match when provided.
bpoa.constraints: Behavior when matching data is unverified.

epoa.description: Ending Period of Availability exact match when provided.
epoa.constraints: Behavior when matching data is unverified.

a.description: Availability Type Code exact match when provided.
a.constraints: Behavior when matching data is unverified.

main.description: Main Account Code exact match when provided.
main.constraints: Behavior when matching data is unverified.

sub.description: Sub Account Code exact match when provided.
sub.constraints: Behavior when matching data is unverified.

filters.constraints: Missing, null, string, or array values for 'filters' fall back to default suggestions. Unknown keys are ignored.

limit.description: Maximum number of suggestions to return.
limit.constraints: Optional. Defaults to 10. Accepts integers from 0 to 500 inclusive; digit strings are coerced to integers. Values above 500 return 422, negative integers return an HTML 500 error, and non-integer types (floats, alphabetic strings, null) return 400.

results.description: Sorted list of ATA matches. May include a trailing placeholder row with null fields when filters allow missing ATAs.

ata.description: Three-digit ATA code when present; null in placeholder rows.

agency_name.description: Full agency name for the ATA; null in placeholder rows.

agency_abbreviation.description: Agency abbreviation; null in placeholder rows.

results.agency_name: Department of Health and Human Services

body.detail: Invalid value in 'limit'. 'abc' is not a valid type (integer)

quirks: Missing, null, array, or string values for 'filters' fall back to default suggestions, and unknown keys are silently ignored.
Digit strings for 'limit' are coerced to integers, but floats and nulls return 400 errors while negative integers trigger an HTML 500 response.
Results are sorted ascending by ATA and can include a placeholder row where all fields are null when filters allow accounts without an ATA.
risks: Negative 'limit' values return an HTML 500 response instead of JSON validation feedback.
Non-object filter payloads or typos silently fall back to default suggestions, masking invalid filter usage.
Placeholder rows with null fields may appear when filters include accounts missing an ATA, requiring client-side null handling.
gaps: Behavior of bpoa, epoa, a, main, and sub filters when matching records is unverified.
Real responses with non-placeholder null agency_abbreviation values have not been observed.

probes.notes: Prefix '02' returns three ATA matches sorted by code.
Limit 100 is accepted; only 19 matching ATAs are returned.
AID filter returns multiple ATAs plus a null placeholder row.
Negative limit values surface an HTML 500 error instead of JSON validation feedback.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Invalid value in 'limit'. 'abc' is not a valid type (integer)"}

probes.notes: Non-numeric string limits fail validation with a 400 error.
Null filters are treated as missing filters and return the default suggestion list.

mismatches: Docs state that 'filters' is required, but the API accepts missing, null, string, or array filters and returns default suggestions.
Docs describe 'limit' as a generic number, but the API enforces an integer-only range of 0-500 and rejects floats.
gaps: Behavior of bpoa, epoa, a, main, and sub filters when matching records is unverified.
Real responses with non-placeholder null agency_abbreviation values have not been observed.
risks: Negative 'limit' values return an HTML 500 response instead of JSON validation feedback.
Non-object filter payloads or typos silently fall back to default suggestions, masking invalid filter usage.
Placeholder rows with null fields may appear when filters include accounts missing an ATA, requiring client-side null handling.
